code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test23-SEP-2020Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, with elevated creatinine suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephropathy and impaired concentrating ability. No evidence of urinary infection. Findings are consistent with chronic kidney disease, likely with tubular dysfunction. Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.
